title: Active Geometric Maintenance of the Spinal S-Curve Against Gravity: An Information–Mechanical Coupling Model for Adolescent Idiopathic Scoliosis Onset
description: Manuscript, reproducible analysis code, and datasets supporting an active-control framework for adolescent idiopathic scoliosis (AIS). Cross-species allometry (n=18 vertebrates), AlphaFold-derived structural metrics across a 61-gene panel, and a delay-differential model of postural control jointly explain why AIS clusters at peak height velocity, in the thoracic spine, and predominantly in girls. Includes the extended Bg-validation simulation sweep (1920 sims) covering humans through dolphins.
